next_steps: Perform serum and CSF testing for Angiostrongylus cantonensis antibody (e.g., ELISA or PCR)
Perform serum and CSF testing for Gnathostoma spinigerum
Send CSF for Coccidioides complement fixation and immunodiffusion
Obtain MRI of the brain and spine with and without gadolinium contrast
Continue dexamethasone to manage intracranial pressure and inflammation
Consult infectious disease and neurology specialists for urgent parasite serology guidance
Monitor intracranial pressure and assess for signs of hydrocephalus
Obtain MRI of the brain and entire spine with and without gadolinium contrast to look for radiculitis, arachnoiditis, or parenchymal lesions
Perform serum and CSF PCR/antibody testing for Angiostrongylus cantonensis
Send serum and CSF for Gnathostoma spinigerum serology
Consult infectious disease and neurology for specialized testing (e.g., Schistosoma and Paragonimus serologies)
Continue systemic corticosteroids for management of eosinophilic inflammation and intracranial pressure
Perform repeat lumbar puncture if neurologic status worsens to monitor pressure and CSF profile
Carefully document dietary history, specifically exposure to raw snails, slugs, or contaminated raw produce
